slides.title: OpenClaw and enterprise AI agents
slides.subtitle: Explain strategic significance, practical boundaries, and enterprise landing path
slides.claim: The practical value comes from controlled execution, not only conversation quality.

slides.title: Deck Logic
slides.subtitle: Frame the shift, define the landing path, then make the control decision explicit
slides.claim: The discussion must move from strategic shift to enterprise landing discipline.

supporting_chapters: How enterprise landing differs from consumer automation
Why control-first architecture is the adoption boundary
blocks.decision_cue: Strategic interest is real, but landing quality depends on controllability.

slides.title: Strategic Framing
slides.claim: The practical value comes from controlled execution, not only conversation quality.

blocks.primary_statement: The practical value comes from controlled execution, not only conversation quality.
support_points: OpenClaw shifted attention from chatbot interaction to action-oriented agent workflows.
Controlled execution, not generic conversation quality, is the decision lever.

slides.title: Executive Summary
slides.claim: Enterprises should treat agents as a control-first execution layer, not unchecked autonomy.

blocks.summary: Agents become valuable when they move from response quality to controlled execution quality.
implications: Research and story must stay structured for repeatable revision.
Style should reinforce decision clarity, not decorate generic templates.
Editable delivery is mandatory for enterprise review loops.
blocks.decision_cue: Build a narrow, editable, control-first MVP before expanding style families.
